---
title: "29 · TD with Function Approximation: Semi-Gradient TD"
subtitle: "Derive the TD update for w via the chain rule; understand why it's semi-gradient, not full SGD."
lesson-id: "mfrl.td-with-function-approximation"
tier: "mfrl"
prerequisites: ["mfrl.from-table-to-function", "t6.gradient-methods"]
unlocks: []
applied-domains: ["ai-ml", "research"]
estimated-minutes: 30
status: "planned"
---

::: {.callout-warning}
**Status: planned (skeleton).** This lesson has been designed but not yet
authored. The full beat-by-beat outline is in the curriculum plan at
`~/.claude/plans/here-are-some-of-steady-aho.md` (the *Mathematics for
Reinforcement Learning* plan). Frontmatter and prerequisites here are the
canonical declaration; the body below is a placeholder that will be
replaced with the six-beat lesson content in a follow-up authoring pass.
:::

::: {.preflight-check}
### Pre-flight check {.mt-0}

This lesson is part of the *Mathematics for Reinforcement Learning*
track. Required prerequisites: ["mfrl.from-table-to-function", "t6.gradient-methods"].

If any of these IDs is unfamiliar, follow the link in
`_curriculum.yml` (or the navbar) before starting.
:::

## Touch

*To be authored. See the lesson outline in the plan file for the
specific Touch widget / concrete object that opens this lesson.*

## Wonder

*To be authored. 2-4 open questions — no answers — that surface
intuition before vocabulary.*

## Discover

*To be authored. 3-7 ordered problems forcing the lesson's central
idea before any naming.*

## Name

*To be authored. Compact formal definition (1-2 sentences) per the
six-beat contract.*

## Apply

*To be authored. Worked example + Warm / Working / Stretch graded
practice with graduated hints and full worked solutions.*

## Connect

### Prerequisites used

["mfrl.from-table-to-function", "t6.gradient-methods"]

### What this unlocks

To be filled in once the next lesson exists; see `_curriculum.yml`
`unlocks` field for the canonical list.

### Where this matters

::: {.applied-callout}
**AI / ML.** This lesson is part of a sequence that derives every
modern RL algorithm from first principles. Real systems built on
this foundation include: every RL agent in production (game-playing
agents, recommendation systems, robotics control loops), and
the algorithms that train them.
:::

::: {.applied-callout}
**Sister synthesis lesson.** [`t7.rl-theory`](../../tier-7-masters-tracks/06-rl-theory/index.qmd)
gives the single-paragraph view of reinforcement learning that this
track expands into 38 lessons.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Author's notes

- Source: Shiyu Zhao, *Mathematical Foundations of Reinforcement
  Learning* (Springer 2025), §29. Cite freely; never transcribe
  Zhao's prose.
- Touchstones honoured: Russian school problem-first sequencing
  (Discover before Name), and Better Explained "Aha!" framing
  (the key-insight callout when authored).
:::
